cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at this question. The question is asking what the author did when he met Mr. Lee. The options are A, B, C, D. The passage starts with the author recounting a memory from when he was ten years old. He was walking with his mother, and they met Mr. Lee. The key part here is that he "didn't pay any attention to him" when they passed Mr. Lee. So, the question is about what the author did. The options are: A. He talked with somebody else. B. He was attracted by a dog. C. He greeted him politely. D. He ignored him. The passage clearly says the author didn't pay attention to Mr. Lee. The mother's lesson was that the author had not spoken to Mr. Lee. The phrase "you let that be the last time you've ever walked by somebody and not opened up your mouth to speak" implies that the author didn't speak to Mr. Lee. So, the correct answer would be D. He ignored him. The other options don't fit. There's no mention of talking to someone else (A), being attracted by a dog (B)
